note: Purge File auto-invalidates file entity URLs/paths through the Purge module on file insert/update/delete. It defines a settings config object, a Purge processor and queuer, and one alter hook. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded) — they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source to answer; no site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, what must already be enabled/configured before Purge File can actually purge anything?
evals.expected_output: The Purge module must be enabled, plus at least one purger that supports a URL-oriented invalidation type (url, wildcardurl, path, or wildcardpath) and at least one purge processor.

evals.prompt: What is the settings route name and admin path for Purge File, and which permission gates it?
evals.expected_output: Route purge_file.settings at /admin/config/development/performance/purge-file, gated by the core 'administer site configuration' permission (the module defines no permissions of its own).

evals.prompt: Which config key on purge_file.settings decides whether invalidations run immediately or go through the Purge queue, and what are its values?
evals.expected_output: The 'workflow' key on purge_file.settings: 'immediate' uses the module's immediate processor (purge_file_immediate_processor); 'queue' adds invalidations via the purge_file_queuer for later processing (e.g. cron).

evals.prompt: How do I make Purge File append a wildcard so all query-string variants of a file URL are purged?
evals.expected_output: Set invalidation_type to wildcardurl (or wildcardpath) on purge_file.settings; when that type is enabled by a purger the module appends '*' to each purge URL. (The old boolean 'wildcard' key was replaced by invalidation_type in update 10002.)

evals.prompt: When a file entity is updated, under what condition does Purge File actually purge, and does it purge the old URL?
evals.expected_output: On update it purges only if the file's URI or size changed; if the new file URL differs from the original's URL it also purges the original file's URL. Temporary-stream (temporary://) files are skipped.

evals.prompt: In a custom module I want to add an extra CDN domain to the URLs Purge File invalidates for each file. Which hook do I implement?
evals.expected_output: Implement hook_purge_file_base_urls_alter(array &$base_urls, FileInterface $file) and append your domain to $base_urls (affects the url/wildcardurl invalidation types).

evals.prompt: My site's public front URL differs from the back-office URL. How do I tell Purge File which host(s) to purge?
evals.expected_output: Set the 'base_urls' key on purge_file.settings to a comma-separated list of base URLs. If left empty the module uses the current request's scheme+host+base path. Used only for url/wildcardurl types.
